title: armola deltoidea
intro: Armola deltoidea, commonly known as False Pashana, is a plant species native to South America and is traditionally used in herbal medicine. The health benefits of Armola deltoidea include its ability to treat various ailments such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The therapeutic actions of this plant include its expectorant and antitussive properties, making it effective in relieving cough and congestion. Bioactive constituents of Armola deltoidea include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
parts: Armola deltoidea has many medicinal parts, such as its leaves, roots, and flowers, which are used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions. The leaves of Armola deltoidea contain flavonoids like kaempferol and quercetin, which have anti-inflammatory properties. The roots of this plant contain alkaloids like berberine, which have antimicrobial and antidiabetic effects. The flowers of Armola deltoidea are rich in volatile oils like limonene and beta-pinene, which are used to treat respiratory issues like bronchitis and coughs.
